emergent_themes.description: Disproportionate negative reaction to small service fees that creates lasting brand perception damage
Disconnect between self-reported and actual ordering frequency, possibly due to social desirability bias
Complete unawareness of available student benefits despite being engaged platform users
Fear that higher subscription costs will psychologically pressure increased ordering behavior

coding_summary: Francesca demonstrates classic price-sensitive student behavior with strong functional food relationships. Her €1 service fee fixation with Just Eat shows how small fees can create disproportionate brand damage. Despite claiming to order 'less than every 3 months' in her profile, she actually orders 1-2 times weekly, suggesting perception gaps around ordering frequency. Most striking is her complete unawareness of Deliveroo's free student subscription despite being an engaged user, highlighting major communication failures. Her post-graduation projections show expected maturation toward more cooking and less delivery usage, with unique insights about subscription control anxiety.

coverage_gaps.reason: Francesca shows no stigma around solo ordering - she prefers it for convenience and doesn't express any negative emotions about ordering alone
